agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Parcel delivery logistics platform managing shipment lifecycle from creation through transit to delivery, with weight-based pricing tiers.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A domain entity representing a parcel to be transported from a pickup address to a delivery address, progressing through defined lifecycle states."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where a parcel is collected from the sender."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where a parcel is delivered to the recipient."},{"term":"Shipment State","definition":"The current lifecycle phase of a shipment: Pending, In Transit, or Delivered."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"A classification of shipments by weight that determines the pricing tier: Standard, Heavy, or Freight."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments under 10 kg (0 < weight < 10)."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments from 10 kg up to but not including 50 kg (10 ≤ weight < 50)."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments of 50 kg and above (50 ≤ weight ≤ 1000)."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address to be created.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without both endpoints is logically incoherent — there is no transport without an origin and destination. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create a shipment missing a pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Shipment state transitions follow a strict forward-only lifecycle: Pending → In Transit → Delivered. No backward transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Backward transitions (e.g., In Transit → Pending) would corrupt operational state and violate the physical reality of logistics workflows. State integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to transition a shipment from In Transit back to Pending must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Delivered is a terminal state — no further state transitions are allowed once a shipment is Delivered.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Delivery is a real-world finality event. Reversing it would break downstream processes (invoicing, proof of delivery, customer notifications). State integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to change the state of a Delivered shipment must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly positive (> 0 kg) and must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Zero or negative weight is physically meaningless. The 1000 kg upper bound represents a physical/operational hard limit of the platform's transport capabilities.","testable_assertion":"Any shipment with weight ≤ 0 or weight > 1000 must be rejected at creation time."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Weight bracket classification: Standard for weight < 10 kg, Heavy for 10 kg ≤ weight < 50 kg, Freight for weight ≥ 50 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Weight bracket thresholds and pricing tiers are business policy decisions that could be adjusted by product or finance leadership without corrupting system integrity.","testable_assertion":"A shipment of 9.99 kg is Standard; 10 kg is Heavy; 49.99 kg is Heavy; 50 kg is Freight; 1000 kg is Freight."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A request to create a shipment with both a valid pickup address and a valid delivery address and a weight of 5 kg","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The shipment is persisted in Pending state with weight bracket Standard"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A request to create a shipment with a missing pickup address","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating pickup address is required"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A request to create a shipment with a missing delivery address","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating delivery address is required"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"It is transitioned to In Transit","then":"The state changes to In Transit successfully"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"An attempt is made to transition it back to Pending","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains In Transit"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"It is transitioned to Delivered","then":"The state changes to Delivered successfully"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A shipment in Delivered state","when":"An attempt is made to change its state to any other state","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains Delivered"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight 0 kg","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight -5 kg","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must be positive"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight 1001 kg","when":"The creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error indicating weight must not exceed 1000 kg"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight 9.99 kg","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The weight bracket is classified as Standard"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight exactly 10 kg","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The weight bracket is classified as Heavy"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight 49.99 kg","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The weight bracket is classified as Heavy"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight exactly 50 kg","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The weight bracket is classified as Freight"},{"id":"AC-15","given":"A request to create a shipment with weight exactly 1000 kg","when":"The shipment is created","then":"The shipment is accepted and the weight bracket is classified as Freight"},{"id":"AC-16","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"An attempt is made to transition it directly to Delivered","then":"The system rejects the transition as it skips the In Transit state"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight at exact boundary of 10 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Heavy (10 kg is the inclusive lower boundary of Heavy bracket)"},{"scenario":"Weight at exact boundary of 50 kg","expected_behavior":"Classified as Freight (50 kg is the inclusive lower boundary of Freight bracket)"},{"scenario":"Weight at maximum of exactly 1000 kg","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Freight"},{"scenario":"Weight of 0.001 kg (very small but positive)","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Standard"},{"scenario":"Pending shipment attempting direct transition to Delivered","expected_behavior":"Rejected — must pass through In Transit first (the spec defines forward-only transitions implying ordered progression)"},{"scenario":"Delivered shipment attempted transition to Delivered again (idempotent re-delivery)","expected_behavior":"Rejected — Delivered is terminal, no state changes permitted including to itself"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Weight bracket classification directly determines pricing. Misclassification at boundaries (especially 10 kg and 50 kg) would cause revenue leakage or overcharging. Correct enforcement is critical to billing accuracy.","operational":"Strict state machine prevents operational chaos — parcels cannot regress to earlier states, ensuring warehouse, driver, and tracking systems remain consistent.","user":"Customers depend on shipment state accuracy for tracking. Terminal Delivered state provides trust that delivery records are immutable and reliable.","risk":"Low overall risk — the spec is clear and internally consistent. Primary risk is boundary precision in weight classification (floating-point edge cases).","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers (senders)","impact":"Pricing transparency depends on correct weight bracket assignment; shipment tracking depends on accurate state transitions."},{"group":"Customers (recipients)","impact":"Delivery state finality provides reliable proof of delivery."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Revenue accuracy depends on correct weight bracket boundaries being enforced exactly as specified."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Forward-only state machine ensures dispatchers have a reliable view of shipment progress."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Immutable Delivered state reduces disputes; clear state rules simplify troubleshooting."}]},"confidence":{"level":90,"reasoning":"The specification is precise and internally consistent. State transitions, weight constraints, and bracket boundaries are all explicitly defined with clear boundary conditions (inclusive/exclusive). The only minor gap is whether Pending → Delivered (skipping In Transit) is allowed, which is strongly implied as disallowed by the ordered lifecycle but not explicitly stated.","high_confidence_areas":["State transition rules (forward-only, Delivered is terminal)","Weight validation (positive, max 1000 kg)","Weight bracket boundaries (explicitly stated inclusive/exclusive)","Required addresses for shipment creation"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether Pending → Delivered skip is explicitly forbidden or just not mentioned (strongly implied by ordered lifecycle)"],"assumptions":["Pending → Delivered direct transition is not allowed — the lifecycle is strictly ordered (Pending → In Transit → Delivered)","Weight is measured in kg with decimal precision","Addresses are non-empty strings; no specific format validation was described","Weight bracket is assigned at creation and does not change"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
